title: Kaizen Chamber Behavior Specification
purpose: Define how Kaizen creates, manages, transitions, and archives focused workstreams (chambers) while preserving continuity and preventing drift.

chamber_philosophy.principle: Chambers should reduce drift, not multiply confusion.
chamber_philosophy.model: A chamber is a bounded context for sustained work. It has a name, a purpose, a scope, continuity anchors, and an output contract. It is the unit of focused execution.
chamber_philosophy.relationship_to_cycles: Chambers span cycles. A chamber opened in C-256 may remain active through C-260. Cycles mark time; chambers mark focus.

chamber_types.description: Active construction of code, systems, or artifacts.
Policy design, incentive modeling, constitutional changes.
Market sweeps, geopolitical scans, systems research.
Repo health, drift correction, naming cleanup, debt reduction.

proposed_to_active.trigger: operator approval or natural continuation from clock-in

active_to_paused.trigger: scope shift, blocked dependency, or operator decision

active_to_closing.trigger: deliverables complete, scope exhausted, or merged into another chamber

continuity_anchors.purpose: Link a chamber to the broader system so work does not become orphaned.

required_anchor_types.description: Which cycle opened this chamber and which cycles it spans.
Which canonical definitions or manifests this chamber depends on.
If this chamber was spawned from another, link to parent.

when_to_spawn: context weight becomes too mixed for a single thread
execution cadence diverges from current scope
a domain needs its own continuity trail
a new workstream has a distinct deliverable set
spawn_requirements: new chamber must have a distinct purpose from existing active chambers
new chamber must define scope and non-goals
new chamber must include at least one continuity anchor
spawning should be logged in the parent chamber

when_to_merge: two chambers converge on the same deliverable
one chamber is a strict subset of another
merge_requirements: surviving chamber absorbs scope of merged chamber
merged chamber's artifacts are linked in surviving chamber
merge event is logged in both

archive_requirements: continuity anchors transferred to successor if applicable
anti_sprawl: do not create chambers just to theatrically rename the same work
archive stale chambers when continuity is preserved
link related chambers through ledger anchors

example_chamber_template.name: micro-agent-signal-v2
example_chamber_template.purpose: Wire micro sub-agent signals into the Signal Engine and GI computation.
example_chamber_template.non_goals: UI changes, identity system, MIC settlement
